lede: A look at February 29 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Rémi Himbert and Benedikt Höwedes, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Flour Massacre
events.desc: The Flour Massacre took place in the Gaza Strip: Israeli forces opened fire on Palestinians waiting for aid amidst the Gaza war, killing over 100 and wounding 750.

events.title: Misha Defonseca
events.desc: Misha Defonseca admits to fabricating her memoir, Misha: A Mémoire of the Holocaust Years, in which she claims to have lived with a pack of wolves in the woods during the Holocaust.

events.title: Jean-Bertrand Aristide
events.desc: Jean-Bertrand Aristide is removed as president of Haiti following a coup.

events.title: Angelina Jolie
events.desc: Angelina Jolie wears a White Marc Bouwer dress to the 76th Academy Awards, which has since been placed on numerous lists for best Oscars fashion.

events.title: Faucett Perú Flight 251
events.desc: Faucett Perú Flight 251 crashes in the Andes; all 123 passengers and crew are killed.

events.title: Siege of Sarajevo
events.desc: The Siege of Sarajevo officially ends.

events.title: 1960 Agadir earthquake
events.desc: The 5.7 Mw Agadir earthquake shakes coastal Morocco with a maximum perceived intensity of X (Extreme), destroying Agadir and leaving 12,000 dead and another 12,000 injured.

events.title: Admiralty Islands
events.desc: The Admiralty Islands are invaded in Operation Brewer, led by American general Douglas MacArthur, in World War II.

events.title: James Madison University
events.desc: James Madison University is founded at Harrisonburg, Virginia, United States as The State Normal and Industrial School for Women by the Virginia General Assembly.

events.title: Queen Anne's War
events.desc: In Queen Anne's War, French forces and Native Americans stage a raid on Deerfield, Massachusetts Bay Colony, killing 56 villagers and taking more than 100 captive.

births.name: Peter Scanavino
births.line: American actor (Law & Order: Special Victims Unit)

births.name: Herman Hollerith
births.line: American statistician and businessman, co-founder of the Computing-Tabulating-Recording Company (died 1929)

deaths.text: Ali Hassan Mwinyi, 2nd President of Tanzania and 3rd President of Zanzibar
Johann Joachim Eschenburg, German historian and critic

observances.text: As a Christian feast day: Saint John Cassian
As a Christian feast day: February 29 in the Orthodox church
Rare Disease Day (in leap years; usually celebrated in common years on February 28)
